clinical_history: 39-yr-old man with chest discomfort. What is the diagnosis and treatment? CLUE: Abnormalities are very subtle!
diagnosis: Acute inferior transmural ischemia/MI. Note very subtle ST elevations and ST straightening in inferior leads with reciprocal ST depressions in I and aVL. Such reciprocal changes are not seen with pericarditis or early repolarization variant. The patient had a high grade proximal right coronary artery lesion. He was treated with a percutaneous transluminal coronary intervention procedure and ruled in for MI.

report.intervals: PR: ~160 ms, QRS: ~80 ms, QTc: ~440 ms
report.findings: Subtle ST-segment elevation and straightening in the inferior leads (II, III, aVF); reciprocal ST-segment depression in leads I and aVL.
report.impression: Acute inferior transmural myocardial infarction (MI) due to a high-grade proximal right coronary artery lesion.
